slides.title: Navigation Without Page Reload 🔗
slides.content:
# Link and NavLink

Using regular `<a>` tags would cause a full page refresh. React Router provides `Link` and `NavLink` components for client‑side navigation.

## Link

`Link` renders an `<a>` tag but intercepts clicks to prevent a full refresh.

```jsx
import { Link } from 'react-router-dom';

function Nav() {
  return (
    <nav>
      <Link to="/">Home</Link>
      <Link to="/about">About</Link>
    </nav>
  );
}
```

## NavLink

`NavLink` is a special version of `Link` that adds styling attributes when it matches the current URL. You can add an `active` class.

```jsx
<NavLink
  to="/about"
  className={({ isActive }) => isActive ? 'active' : ''}
>
  About
</NavLink>
```

slides.title: NavLink with Active Style
slides.content: Using a function to set className:

quizData.question: What is the advantage of using `Link` over an `<a href>`?

options.text: It prevents a full page refresh

quizData.explanation: `Link` intercepts the click and uses client‑side navigation, avoiding a full page reload.
